frameworks.name: Bayesian function learning
frameworks.one_line: Posterior inference over functions defined on graph-structured input spaces.

frameworks.name: reward learning / exploration-exploitation search
frameworks.one_line: Sampling for rewards extended from continuous to graph-correlated value structures.

core_question: How do people learn functions and search for rewards on graph-structured spaces with correlated values?
key_assumptions: Value structure over options is correlated according to a graph
Human function learning can be modeled as Bayesian inference over functions
The same generative belief supports both prediction and reward-directed sampling
manipulations: Underlying graph structure governing value correlations
Number of options available to the participant
key_findings: Proposed Bayesian model fits predictions and sampling behavior better than alternative models
Model reproduces human-like learning curves on graph-structured tasks
Model also captures participants' confidence judgements
interpretations: Human function learning and reward search generalize to complex graph-structured domains under a shared Bayesian account
